title: dysoxylum acutangulum subsp. foveolatum
intro: Dysoxylum acutangulum subsp. foveolatum, also known as the Philippine dysoxylum or foveolated Philippine dysoxylum, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae. It is a tree native to the Philippines, and its bark has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and other respiratory issues. The bark of the Dysoxylum acutangulum subsp. foveolatum contains flavonoids and tannins, which are known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing relief from coughs, colds, and other infections. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark, such as tea infusions and decoctions, are used to treat various ailments, showcasing its potential as a natural remedy for promoting overall health and well-being.
